note: Seed entries — one per worked problem. These are the harness's REFERENCE BASELINES (hand-authored worked examples committed in this repo), not the output of a live model run. They establish the bar; model-produced runs register their own entries naming the model and linking their own public run repo. Every metric below is the judge's own emitted number (run scoreboard/verify.py to re-derive). model_usage is stated explicitly per entry.
model_usage: For these seeds, model = 'reference-baseline' (no autonomous model produced them; they are the committed worked examples). When you do a run, set model to what you actually pointed at the BRIEF (e.g. 'opus-4.8', 'fable-5'); the model is provenance only and never a ranking key — the judge re-simulates regardless of author.

entries.paradigm: chain-cascade (H + nearest-neighbour CX ladder on a line)
entries.model_usage: hand-authored worked example — the bar to beat, not a model run
entries.why_it_scores: Perfect fidelity (1.000) at the minimal cost for a 3-qubit GHZ state on the [0-1-2] coupling map: depth 3, two CX. No design reaches the target with fewer entangling gates, so this is the optimum — only a tie at lower cost could outrank it.

entries.paradigm: minimal Bell ansatz (H + CX) — exact ground state
entries.model_usage: hand-authored worked example — the bar to beat, not a model run
entries.why_it_scores: Reaches the EXACT ground state (gap 0.000 to E0 = -2) at depth 2 with a single CX. The Bell state is the true ground state of H = -X0X1 - Z0Z1, and entangling beats the best product-state baseline (-1). You cannot improve on a zero gap — only a tie at lower cost competes.

entries.model_usage: hand-authored worked example — the bar to beat, not a model run
entries.why_it_scores: Matches the visible 50/50 populations AND the HIDDEN held-out ⟨X0X1⟩ = +1 — the genuine |Φ+>, not a phase-flipped impostor that games only the visible spec. It clears the anti-overfit gate (exit 6) that the OVERFIT fixture fails, at depth 2 with one CX.

entries.model_usage: hand-authored worked example — the bar to beat, not a model run
entries.why_it_scores: A ring routes BOTH the visible and the held-out workload at cost 2 within the degree-2 budget, beating the linear-chain baseline (4). It generalizes — the overfit path that aces the visible pairs blows the held-out budget and is rejected at exit 6. Only a sparser map at equal cost could outrank it.

entries.model_usage: hand-authored worked example — the bar to beat, not a model run
entries.why_it_scores: 100% train AND 100% held-out test accuracy with a single rotation. A high-frequency Ry(7x) map also nails training but fails the held-out test (exit 6); this low-frequency map generalizes — the headline metric. One op on one qubit: nothing simpler generalizes.

entries.paradigm: hardware-efficient ansatz (Ry-Ry-CX-Ry-Ry) for the H₂ ground state
entries.model_usage: hand-authored worked example — the bar to beat, not a model run
entries.why_it_scores: Reaches within 4e-4 Ha of the exact H₂ ground energy (E0 = -1.8512) with a single CX, capturing the correlation energy the mean-field product state (-1.8302) misses — a real molecular-VQE problem, not a toy. Headroom is open: a deeper or cleverer ansatz that closes the remaining gap, or ties it at lower cost, outranks this baseline.
